title: Multidimensional Regulatory Fit and Anticipated Activity Enjoyment
abstract: The purpose of this study is to test for simultaneous effects of regulatory fit with respect to multiple motivational dimensions on the extent to which students anticipate that they would enjoy various strategies for earning a high GPA that contain activities from Nakkawita and Higgins' (2021) 2 (promotion vs. prevention) x 2 (truth vs. control) goal pursuit activity framework. To do so, after presenting participants with an exploratory motive measure, it will experimentally induce one of two motivational concerns (truth vs. control from Higgins' (2012) theory of motivational effectiveness). Next, participants will be presented with several strategies that can be used to attain the goal of “earning a high GPA”, and will rate how enjoyable each strategy would be to carry out. Then, participants will complete the Regulatory Focus Questionnaire, which will allow us to assess participants' chronic promotion (vs. prevention) pride predominance. We predict that we will detect simultaneous effects of regulatory fit on activity enjoyment from both sets of motivational dimensions (promotion vs. prevention and truth vs. control).

title: Multidimensional Regulatory Fit and Activity Accessibility
abstract: The purpose of this study is to test for simultaneous effects of regulatory fit with respect to multiple motivational dimensions on the mental accessibility of activities from Nakkawita and Higgins' (2021) 2 (promotion vs. prevention) x 2 (truth vs. control) goal pursuit activity framework. To do so, it will measure the strength of one set of motives (promotion vs. prevention from Higgins' (1997) regulatory focus theory) and then experimentally induce a motivational concern from a different set of motives (truth vs. control from Higgins' (2012) theory of motivational effectiveness). Next, after an initial presentation of various goal pursuit activities within an importance-rating task, and then a brief distractor task, we will assess the accessibility of the same goal pursuit activities by examining the output primacy of these activities within an unaided recall task. We predict that we will detect simultaneous effects of regulatory fit on both sets of motivational dimensions.

title: Leveraging Regulatory Fit to Enhance Engagement: A Public-Sector Study in South Korea
abstract: This study extends the Job Demands-Resources (JD-R) model by incorporating regulatory focus (promotion vs. prevention) as a moderator, examining its impact on job engagement among public sector employees (n=306). The results show that employees with a promotion focus are more likely to experience increased engagement when facing challenge stressors, as they perceive such stressors as growth opportunities. Conversely, employees with a prevention focus show reduced negative effects from hindrance stressors, as they seek stability and are more focused on avoiding risks. The findings highlight the importance of regulatory fit in job design, suggesting that aligning job demands with individual motivational tendencies can improve engagement. Promotion-focused employees benefit from opportunities for leadership and innovation, while prevention-focused employees thrive in structured environments prioritizing risk management. Tailored training programs in areas like stress management and communication can further support these employees, enhancing engagement and overall organizational performance.

title: Leveraging Regulatory Fit to Enhance Engagement: A Public-Sector Study
abstract: This survey is designed to explore the culture and characteristics of the organization you're a part of.

title: Message Framing, Regulatory Fit, and Physical-Cognitive-Emotional Health Themes in Digital Health
abstract: This study is about message framing (gain-nonloss), regulatory fit (framing based on regulatory focus, see Higgins, 2001; Ludolph & Schulz, 2015), matching based on concerns and perceived importance of physical, cognitive, and emotional health.

title: Message Framing, Regulatory fit, and Message Length in Digital Mental Health
abstract: We will investigate the effects of personalized message framing (gain, nonloss) in digital mental health. See the pre-registration for details.

title: Examining the moderating role of regulatory fit on stereotype threat among Ugandan adolescents.
abstract: Stereotype threat and regulatory fit among Ugandan adolescents. Published in the Journal of Social Psychology.
